claim: The YOLO demand evidence dispatch system implements JSON drift repair and fail-closed validation as specified in the problem statement and acceptance criteria.

evidence.summary: parseJsonCandidate function implements JSON repair logic for minor Claude key drift
evidence.why: This function is the core repair mechanism that handles common Claude JSON formatting issues.

evidence.summary: Repairs line range format (42-88 → quoted string), escape sequences, and removes extra quotes in keys
evidence.why: These specific repair patterns address common Claude JSON output issues while tracking whether repair occurred.

evidence.summary: extractJsonObject function provides multiple fallback strategies for JSON extraction from provider output
evidence.why: This ensures JSON is found even when embedded in markdown fences or surrounded by other text.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult function handles invalid JSON with EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON error code and prevents completion
evidence.why: This is the fail-closed logic that blocks PRD readiness when agent JSON is unrecoverable.

evidence.summary: Assigns EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON error code when parsed is null
evidence.why: This is the explicit blocker code required by the acceptance criteria.

evidence.summary: Runtime extracts JSON from provider output and normalizes the result, recording repair status
evidence.why: This connects the repair logic to the actual agent execution flow and tracks json_repaired.

evidence.summary: Runtime inspects PRD readiness after agent execution and merges blockers including EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON
evidence.why: This ensures PRD readiness stays blocked when invalid JSON occurs, meeting the acceptance criteria.

evidence.summary: Test 'repairs minor Claude JSON key drift and records the repair' validates the repair behavior
evidence.why: This test confirms the system records repairs and continues processing for recoverable JSON drift.

evidence.summary: Test 'invalid agent JSON fails closed with explicit blocker code' validates the fail-closed behavior
evidence.why: This test confirms that EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON blocks PRD readiness when JSON is unrecoverable.

evidence.summary: Test simulates Claude JSON drift with line ranges, escape sequences, and extra quotes
evidence.why: This demonstrates the exact drift patterns the system is designed to handle.

evidence.summary: Test asserts that every agent result has error_code EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON when JSON is invalid
evidence.why: This confirms the explicit blocker requirement from the acceptance criteria.

risks: If Claude introduces new JSON drift patterns beyond the current repair logic, agents might fail with EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON more frequently

result.notes: The implementation exactly matches the specified behavior: minor recoverable JSON drift is repaired and recorded, unrecoverable JSON receives EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, and PRD readiness stays blocked.
